headline: CONFIRMATION with positive in-window evidence. April 17 Google DeepMind published third iteration of Frontier Safety Framework — introduces (1) Tracked Capability Levels (TCLs) for early less-extreme risk identification, (2) new Critical Capability Level for harmful manipulation in high-stakes contexts, (3) misalignment risk protocols extended to large-scale internal deployments (not just external launches), (4) sharpened CCL definitions and risk-assessment documentation. Positive INT/SYS signal preceding EU AI Act enforcement (96 days, August 2 2026). However: framework lacks specified independent external oversight bodies and detailed deployment thresholds — limits ACC dimension upgrade. Live composite 65.0 (Established band) already reflects strong baseline. Net effect: holds at 65.0; modest positive INT/SYS calibration (+0.2 each) available but does not cross 5-point composite threshold. Note: Google parent entity also affected by April 28 LA jury verdict in addiction case (Meta + Google jointly liable for $6M).

evidence.finding: Third iteration of Frontier Safety Framework: introduces Tracked Capability Levels (TCLs); new Critical Capability Level for harmful manipulation in high-stakes contexts; misalignment risk protocols apply to large-scale internal deployments; sharpened CCL definitions; collaboration with industry/academia/government emphasized.
External coverage frames update as proactive pre-EU AI Act safety commitment.
LA jury found Google + Meta jointly liable in $6M addiction case — partially negative ACC/BND signal for Alphabet parent (impact on DeepMind subdivision indirect).

methodologyNote: DeepMind never previously assessed individually (rotation_state.last_assessed = null). The April 17 FSF v3 is a positive substantive event but composite 65.0 (rank top-tier in AI Labs) already reflects strong baseline. A dedicated full evidence-based assessment cycle is recommended to establish individual scoring rather than relying on confirm/calibration. Confidence raised to HIGH because the FSF publication is a primary-sourced authoritative positive signal.
